page: Microsoft Azure D32lds_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the DLDSv5 family. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz, featuring 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs via hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is supported by a 1229 GB local disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results place the D32lds_v5 in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. The system achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 1674.0 and a multi-core score of 14190.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 36820.34. It demonstrates strong performance in specific workloads, such as Geekbench image processing and small-model LLM text generation. However, memory bandwidth for large blocks drops to 148.96 GB/sec for read and 65.75 GB/sec for write operations.

The primary tradeoff of the D32lds_v5 is its low memory-to-vCPU ratio, which limits its suitability for memory-intensive applications. This is reflected in its poor performance on large-scale LLM inference, where it manages only 2.29 tokens/sec on a 70B model. Qualitatively, this server offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive RAM footprints, leveraging its dedicated Intel Xeon cores and local storage. It is well-suited for web serving, software compilation, image processing, and moderate database operations.
description: Microsoft Azure D32lds_v5 is an x86_64 server instance from the DLDSv5 family, configured with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of memory and a 1229 GB local disk. Benchmarks show average performance across standard compute tasks, with a Passmark CPU score of 36820.34 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 14190.0. It exhibits strong performance in image processing and small-model LLM text generation, though it struggles with large-scale LLM inference due to memory constraints. This instance represents a cost-efficient option for compute-centric workloads that require local storage but have low memory requirements, such as web serving, software compilation, and moderate database tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32lds_v5 is an x86_64 server with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 1229 GB local disk. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C CPU, it is optimized for compute-heavy workloads with low memory requirements.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32lds_v5 is a 32-vCPU x86_64 server featuring 64 GB RAM, 1229 GB local storage, and an Intel Xeon 8370C processor for compute-focused tasks.
tagline: A compute-focused x86_64 server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, local storage, and a low memory-to-core ratio for cost-efficient processing.

Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM and a 1229 GB local storage disk
Strong performance in image processing and small-model LLM text generation
